mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stays above 4.0% for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in core inflation and the federal funds rate indicate aggressive monetary tightening that suppresses aggregate demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns during early economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in over 50% of rising INDPRO periods, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales volumes indicate robust consumer spending and immediate corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of high retail sales observations, or the correlation decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and pushes equity markets higher.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in over 50% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index surpasses 310, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in over 60% of periods where CPI exceeds 310, or the threshold effect disappears after adjusting for base year reindexing.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in new housing starts signal renewed business investment and construction sector activity, which amplifies economic momentum and lifts S&P 500 returns during post-recession recoveries.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to predict positive returns in over 55% of recovery periods, or the signal becomes negative when mortgage rates exceed 7%.
